task_completion.description: Verify that the required layers with correct colors are created in LibreCAD.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the LibreCAD Layer List panel, there should be layers named 'PIPE_CENTERLINE' with a red color swatch, 'FITTINGS' with a cyan color swatch, and 'TEXT' with a white/black color swatch.

task_completion.description: Verify the continuous pipe run is drawn on the correct layer with the correct isometric shape.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A continuous red line should be visible in the drawing area. The shape should consist of four segments: starting with a long vertical line going UP, followed by a line angling UP-LEFT, then a line angling UP-RIGHT, and finishing with a short vertical line going DOWN.

task_completion.description: Verify the circular fittings are drawn at the correct junctions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two cyan circles should be visible: a larger circle at the junction between the first vertical segment and the up-left angled segment, and a smaller circle at the very end of the final downward vertical segment.

task_completion.description: Verify the text labels are added to the drawing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: White text reading 'RISER' should be visible near the first vertical line, and 'DN 100' should be visible near the main horizontal/angled run.

task_completion.description: Verify the file is saved with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar or a save dialog should display the filename 'fire_riser_iso.dxf'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually used the LibreCAD GUI to create the drawing.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LibreCAD interface with active tool selections, command line inputs at the bottom of the LibreCAD window, or layer panel interactions.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use a terminal script to generate the DXF file directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands executing Python scripts (like ezdxf) or echoing raw DXF text to bypass the CAD software.

integrity.description: Ensure the drawing is in the actual CAD workspace.
integrity.visual_evidence: The drawing should be overlaid on the LibreCAD grid with CAD crosshairs or selection handles visible, not just an image opened in a picture viewer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The drawing uses standard isometric projection angles: 90° (vertical up), 150° (isometric left), 30° (isometric right), and 270° (vertical down). Layer colors correspond to standard AutoCAD Color Index values: Red (1) for PIPE_CENTERLINE, Cyan (4) for FITTINGS, and White (7) for TEXT. The final file must be saved exactly as /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/fire_riser_iso.dxf.
